reconciliation.scope_note: lifecycle counts are cumulative since canonical accounting began; baseline counts are legacy ledger totals that predate it; ledger/gate totals == baseline + accounted.

execution_gate_zero_reject_diagnostic.message: execution gate rejected 0 of 9 candidates that reached it — verify this is liquidity reality, not a disabled gate
likely_explanations: Polymarket BTC 5m books are tight + deep, so spread/depth/partial-fill checks pass
VWAP slippage over the top of book is tiny at $5.0 size, so EV-after-cost stays positive
the directional stage already rejected 354 candidates before the gate, so only execution-feasible candidates reached it

tradingview.note: TradingView alerts are candidate signals only — they cannot place, resize, or bypass trades; the strategy + execution gate remain the sole trade authority.

edge_vs_5min_outcome.note: observe-only: did the TradingView signal at entry predict the 5-min Chainlink outcome (signal_hit_rate vs baseline_up_rate), and did aligning help the bot win (aligned vs opposed bot_win_rate)? Never affects trading.

rsi_trend.note: observe-only: learns P(next 5-min outcome | RSI alert trend state) from the alert history and scores its own next-window predictions. Never trades.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

strong_fade.rule: reject entry when fresh signal_level matches {SIDE}_STRONG

signal_gate.note: when active, a paper trade is taken only if a fresh TradingView signal agrees with the side; it can only PREVENT trades, never force or bypass them.

context_gate.note: hard prior gate: blocks proven-losing entry contexts (volume spikes, noise regime, far-from-resolution) before enough samples exist for the learned selectivity gate. Can only PREVENT trades — never forces, sizes, or bypasses the execution gate, which remains the sole trade authority.

down_bias_gate.note: restrict-only: harvest DOWN/TV asymmetry by blocking proven-losing UP stacks

mtf_gate.note: observe-only conflict veto: blocks 4m/5m fast-pair conflict only (loop arch default). require_confirm/side_align are opt-in restrict-only layers, not trade authority.

confidence_tier.note: At 15m TTC sweet spot, TV MTF regime adjusts min_edge/max_price only. TV trade gates remain off per operator lock.

open_regime.note: Early-arrive alerts (0–15m): open-regime / price-path trend; not an entry trigger.

actionable_trend.note: In-band alerts (15–45m): entry-time confirmation; highest weight for decisions.

lookback_tail.note: Full 2h lookback incl. late-arrive alerts — study completeness, not a hard gate.

post_band.note: Late-arrive alerts (45–60m): complete prior-hour story for 2h study; never veto entry.

BTCUSD.note: 2h TV alert trend vs price path — segmented by hourly phase (early=regime, in-band=decision, late=2h study). Observe-only; not a trade gate unless pretrade/council flags are enabled.

open_regime.note: Early-arrive alerts (0–15m): open-regime / price-path trend; not an entry trigger.

actionable_trend.note: In-band alerts (15–45m): entry-time confirmation; highest weight for decisions.

focus.note: 2h TV alert trend vs price path — segmented by hourly phase (early=regime, in-band=decision, late=2h study). Observe-only; not a trade gate unless pretrade/council flags are enabled.

price_pattern.note: Plot short_path (oldest→newest OHLC) as the current price pattern; regime_path_tail is HTF context only. RSI is separate overlay.

tradingview_15m_price_path.note: Rolling FIFO last 50 alerts/symbol (5m bar-close preferred on *USDT): regime chart for Grok; last 8 = short-term trade lean / price pattern.

tradingview_official.source: TradingView Help Center — Relative Strength Index (RSI)
tradingview_official.author: J. Welles Wilder Jr. (New Concepts in Technical Trading Systems, 1978)
tradingview_official.rsi_scale: 0–100 momentum oscillator; closer to 0 = weak momentum, closer to 100 = strong
tradingview_official.formula_summary: RSI = 100 - 100/(1 + RS); RS = avg gain / avg loss over n bars
tradingview_official.built_in_divergence_setting: TradingView's built-in RSI has 'Calculate Divergence' checkbox in Inputs — highlights where RSI direction diverges from price (bullish/bearish flag).
tradingview_official.wilder_divergence_definition: RSI Divergence = price action disagrees with RSI. Interpret as impending reversal.
tradingview_official.wilder_bullish_divergence: Price makes a new low but RSI makes a higher low → buying opportunity (Wilder).
tradingview_official.wilder_bearish_divergence: Price makes a new high but RSI makes a lower high → selling opportunity (Wilder).
tradingview_official.cardwell_trend_context: Andrew Cardwell: bullish divergence usually only in bearish trends; bearish divergence only in bullish trends. Divergence often causes a brief correction, not a full trend reversal — use to confirm trends, not only anticipate reversals.
tradingview_official.cardwell_positive_reversal: Bullish trend only: price makes higher low while RSI makes lower low — price proceeds to rise (momentum lags price).
tradingview_official.cardwell_negative_reversal: Bearish trend only: price makes lower high while RSI makes higher high — price proceeds to fall (momentum lags price).
tradingview_official.divergence_limitations: TradingView: divergence is lagging, not always at reversals, combine with other tools — never rely on divergence alone.

bullish: RSI pulls back but stays above 30
RSI breaks above its previous high
bearish: RSI rises slightly but stays below 70
RSI drops below its previous low
failure_swings.note: Failure swings are RSI-only (independent of price); distinct from pivot divergence.

operator_indicator.name: Hermes RSI Divergence Indicator
operator_indicator.chart_symbols: 1h lane: BINANCE:BTCUSDT + BINANCE:ETHUSDT · 5m; 15m lane: INDEX:BTCUSD + INDEX:ETHUSD · 5m (Chainlink settlement oracle)

config.webhook_hidden: plot only — not sent to bot

operator_indicator.detection_logic: Finds RSI pivot lows/highs (ta.pivotlow/pivothigh on RSI with lbL=5, lbR=5). Compares consecutive pivots within 5–60 bars: price swing vs RSI swing must disagree.

primer.definition: RSI divergence: price pivots and RSI pivots disagree on momentum. TradingView/Wilder: bullish = price lower low + RSI higher low; bearish = price higher high + RSI lower high. Our Pine script uses pivot lookback 5/5 and 5–60 bar range between pivots.

regular_bullish.pattern: price lower low + RSI higher low
regular_bullish.pine_checks: priceLL = low < prior pivot low; oscHL = RSI > prior pivot RSI low
regular_bullish.meaning: Selling pressure weakening despite new price low — RSI does not confirm the low. Wilder: buying opportunity; strongest near oversold (<30) or after bearish stretch.
regular_bullish.typical_zone: Often fires from oversold/neutral RSI; stronger when RSI was <40

regular_bearish.pattern: price higher high + RSI lower high
regular_bearish.pine_checks: priceHH = high > prior pivot high; oscLH = RSI < prior pivot RSI high
regular_bearish.meaning: Buying pressure weakening despite new price high — RSI does not confirm the high. Wilder: selling opportunity; strongest near overbought (>70) or after bullish stretch.
regular_bearish.typical_zone: Often fires from overbought/neutral RSI; stronger when RSI was >60

hidden_bullish.pattern: price higher low + RSI lower low
hidden_bullish.pine_checks: priceHL = low > prior pivot low; oscLL = RSI < prior pivot RSI low
hidden_bullish.meaning: Trend-continuation UP — pullback held, momentum cooled but trend may resume

hidden_bearish.pattern: price lower high + RSI higher high
hidden_bearish.pine_checks: priceLH = high < prior pivot high; oscHH = RSI > prior pivot RSI high
hidden_bearish.meaning: Trend-continuation DOWN — rally faded, RSI firm but price failed higher

primer.vs_rsi_band: rsi_band = continuous 30/70 Wilder zones every bar (mean-revert backdrop). rsi_divergence = sparse pivot event when price vs RSI pivots disagree. Use band for zone context; divergence for exhaustion timing confirm/fade.
primer.bot_usage: Observe-only confirm/fade overlay on regular bull/bear only: aligned divergence → size up; opposed → size down. Never overrides price_path trend. Cardwell: treat divergence as correction/trend-confirm context on 5m BTC/ETH, not guaranteed reversal.
primer.how_to_read_alerts: REGULAR_BULL_DIV / regular_bullish → UP lean. REGULAR_BEAR_DIV / regular_bearish → DOWN lean. Check rsi field + rsi_zone_at_signal. Sparse — no alert between pivots is normal.

tradingview_rsi_divergence.note: No RSI divergence alerts in FIFO yet — indicator fires on pivot disagreement only.

learning.note: the bot's own settled-trade experience (calibrated edge model) adjusts the directional probability; it grows as more trades settle. The execution-quality gate, paper-realism, and reconciliation are unchanged and still veto every trade — learning can never bypass them.

grok_signal_intel.note: A analyzes signal-learning performance; B predicts P(up) per signal and is graded vs realized moves. Both observe-only — never place/size/bypass a trade; the execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

aggression.note: loosens (more explore/looser exploit/larger size) as acted trades profit; tightens on losses; circuit breaker is the hard floor.

grok_decider.note: Grok decider is observe-only (shadow): decide+grade per window; never places or sizes trades. Fail-closed -> no_trade in the advisory payload.

loops.note: formalized sub-loops (data/signal/verify/execute/risk/research/news/lessons); each has a trigger + skill + verifier + verifiable stop + a liveness watchdog (last_beat_age_s/stalled). PAPER ONLY.

promotion.note: observe-only diagnostic; eligible buckets are NOT auto-promoted to trading authority unless promotion_allowed_by_config is true AND explicitly wired. The execution gate remains the sole trade authority.

baseline_cohort_gate.note: baseline quant path: 180-240s TTC band (scaled on 15m), high edge + strong CEX; UP blocked until promoted; green_path=15m DOWN cohort only (TV observe-only)

learned_selectivity_gate.note: rejects ONLY buckets confidently below their own breakeven win-rate (Wilson upper bound < breakeven AND net-negative PnL); coin-flip / not-significant buckets are NOT rejected. Can only make the bot MORE selective — never trades, resizes, or bypasses the execution gate. Exploration tracked separately.

bucket_evidence.rule: blocked iff confidently_losing AND fdr_significant (Wilson upper < breakeven, PF < min, BH-FDR q=0.10)

counterfactual.note: in-sample replay using final accumulated bucket evidence (diagnostic estimate)

gate.note: when enabled, only late-window AND high-conviction setups may trade; can only PREVENT trades, never force/size/bypass the execution gate.

edge_measurement.note: observe-only: grades whether late-window high-conviction trades win more (cohort vs other) from live settled trades; never affects trading.

stop_conditions.note: quantitative kill switches checked each tick from settled ledger evidence; NOT the LLM's opinion. Halting blocks NEW entries only.

execution_realistic_edge.note: KL divergence is observe-only — large KL means model disagrees with market; never a buy signal when model Brier > market Brier.

baseline.note: no legacy ledger — accounting started clean; absorbed 1 fill(s) missing from lifecycle persistence; absorbed 1 fill(s) missing from lifecycle persistence; absorbed 1 fill(s) missing from lifecycle persistence; absorbed 1 fill(s) missing from lifecycle persistence; absorbed 1 fill(s) missing from lifecycle persistence; absorbed 1 fill(s) missing from lifecycle persistence; absorbed 1 fill(s) missing from lifecycle persistence; absorbed 1 fill(s) missing from lifecycle persistence; absorbed 1 fill(s) missing from lifecycle persistence

signal_edge.note: FADE = confidently anti-predictive (Wilson upper < breakeven) -> trade the inverse (gated, never auto); FOLLOW = Wilson lower > breakeven; else OBSERVE. Measured on real settled outcomes; never places/sizes/bypasses a trade.

trading_performance.note: Profitability, win-rate, side balance (DOWN>UP), and ledger integrity.

operation.note: Engine health: reconciliation, loops, stops, and pipeline activity.

external_signals.note: External signal quality and how well signals align with bot outcomes.

proposals.observation: Lifecycle: 5321 candidates created, 0 accepted — directional is still collecting no data.
proposals.hypothesis: A gate downstream of the WS2 exploration (verifier veto / underdog floor / down-bias) is still blocking every candidate.
proposals.proposed_change: Check rejected_by_stage; if execution-gate/verifier dominate, wire explore_approve or relax the underdog floor quota for exploration.
proposals.evidence_gate: Need accepted>0 and settled>0 before any bucket can be judged.

proposals.observation: 5x not proven (improvement_ratio 0.572); primary edge source: directional.
proposals.hypothesis: Total P&L is below the 5x baseline; the proven lane should get the capital.
proposals.proposed_change: Concentrate sizing/iteration on the primary edge source (directional) and park unproven lanes.
proposals.evidence_gate: improvement_ratio must trend up across soaks with the proven lane leading.

loop_synthesis.summary: 2 proposal(s); 1 high-priority. Top: [directional] Check rejected_by_stage; if execution-gate/verifier dominate, wire explore_approve or relax the underdog floor quota for exploration.
loop_synthesis.note: Advisory next-experiment proposals from live performance. Paper-only; never edits config or trades. Each names the evidence gate that would justify acting.
